questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light changes
Slow down and proceed with caution
Yield to traffic on the left only
Stop only if other cars are present
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to come to a full stop and stay stopped until the signal turns green.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal should be treated the same as which sign?
choices: A stop sign
A yield sign
A do-not-enter sign
A speed-limit sign
questions.explanation: A flashing red signal means come to a complete stop and proceed only when it is safe, just like a stop sign.

questions.prompt: What shape and color is a stop sign?
choices: A red octagon
A yellow diamond
A white rectangle
A green triangle
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red eight-sided octagon, a shape used for no other sign.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is broken and should be ignored
Pedestrians have the right of way only
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that red is coming next, so you should stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What does a yellow diamond-shaped sign indicate?
choices: A warning about conditions ahead
A mandatory stop
The end of a speed zone
A destination or distance
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert you to possible hazards or changes in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center line means:
choices: Do not pass
Passing is allowed
The road is one-way
You may park here
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because it is not safe to do so.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic signal mean?
choices: Proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop
Yield only to pedestrians
The intersection is closed
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means slow down and proceed carefully, watching for cross traffic.

questions.prompt: What do white lane lines on a roadway separate?
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
Lanes of traffic moving in opposite directions
The road from the shoulder only
Parking spaces from travel lanes
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic that travel in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: An orange sign or cone on the road most often indicates:
choices: A construction or work zone
A school crossing
A no-parking area
A scenic route
questions.explanation: Orange is used for signs and devices in construction and maintenance work zones.

questions.prompt: A downward-pointing triangular sign means:
choices: Yield
Stop
No entry
Merge
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is always a yield sign, telling you to slow down and give the right of way.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
choices: The vehicle on the right
The vehicle on the left
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are making a left turn at a green light without a green arrow, you must:
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians
Proceed immediately because you have the right of way
Stop and wait for the light to turn red
Honk and turn quickly
questions.explanation: A left turn on a green light must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing your path.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian is in a crosswalk, you must:
choices: Stop and stay stopped until they have crossed
Honk to warn them
Drive around them carefully
Proceed if you have a green light
questions.explanation: Pedestrians in a crosswalk have the right of way, and drivers must stop and remain stopped for them.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on, you should:
choices: Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the way
Stop immediately in your lane
Turn left into a side street
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: At an intersection without signs or signals, you should yield to:
choices: Vehicles that have already entered the intersection
Only vehicles on your left
Larger vehicles only
No one, because you arrived first
questions.explanation: You must yield to vehicles that are already in the intersection before you proceed.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must yield to:
choices: Traffic already circulating in the roundabout
Traffic waiting to enter behind you
No one if you signal
Pedestrians only after you exit
questions.explanation: Vehicles already in a roundabout have the right of way over vehicles entering it.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal?
choices: At least 100 feet before turning
Only as you begin to turn
About 10 feet before turning
Signaling is optional
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers time to react to your turn.

questions.prompt: At a T-intersection without signals, who has the right of way?
choices: Traffic on the through road
Traffic on the road that ends
Whoever arrives first
The vehicle turning left
questions.explanation: A vehicle on the road that ends at a T-intersection must yield to traffic on the continuing through road.

questions.prompt: Before turning right, you should position your vehicle:
choices: As close as practical to the right curb or edge
In the center of the lane
In the left lane
Wherever there is space
questions.explanation: Turning from close to the right edge keeps the turn tight and prevents others from passing on the right.

questions.prompt: When you want to merge onto a highway from an entrance ramp, you should:
choices: Adjust your speed to match traffic and merge into a gap
Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
Force your way into the nearest lane
Merge slowly regardless of traffic speed
questions.explanation: Matching the speed of highway traffic lets you blend into a gap smoothly and safely.

questions.prompt: In New Hampshire, what is the default speed limit in an urban or business district unless posted otherwise?
choices: 30 mph
45 mph
55 mph
20 mph
questions.explanation: The default limit in business and urban residence districts is 30 mph unless a sign posts a different speed.

questions.prompt: What is the recommended minimum following distance under good conditions?
choices: Three seconds
One second
One car length
Ten feet
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives you enough time and space to react to the vehicle ahead under ideal conditions.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or fog, you should adjust your following distance by:
choices: Increasing it to four seconds or more
Keeping it the same
Reducing it to stay close
Tailgating to see better
questions.explanation: Slippery or low-visibility conditions require more space because stopping distances increase.

questions.prompt: A posted speed limit represents:
choices: The maximum legal speed under ideal conditions
The minimum speed you must drive
A suggested speed you may exceed
The average speed of traffic
questions.explanation: Speed limits are the maximum allowed when conditions are good; you must slow down when conditions are poor.

questions.prompt: When approaching a work zone, you should:
choices: Slow down and be prepared to stop
Maintain your speed
Speed up to clear it quickly
Change lanes at the last second
questions.explanation: Reducing speed in work zones protects workers and gives you time to respond to sudden changes.

questions.prompt: Even when you are at the posted speed limit, you must slow down when:
choices: Road, weather, or traffic conditions are poor
There is no other traffic
You are running late
The road is straight
questions.explanation: The law requires you to drive at a speed that is reasonable and prudent for actual conditions.

questions.prompt: On most rural highways in New Hampshire, the default speed limit unless posted higher is:
choices: 55 mph
65 mph
40 mph
30 mph
questions.explanation: Outside of districts, the default limit is 55 mph unless a higher limit is posted on certain highways.

questions.prompt: If another driver is tailgating you, the safest response is to:
choices: Increase your following distance from the car ahead
Brake quickly to warn them
Speed up to lose them
Slam on your brakes
questions.explanation: Leaving more room in front gives you space to brake gradually so the tailgater can also stop in time.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because:
choices: It can block traffic and cause crashes
It always saves fuel
It is required in all lanes
It improves your reaction time
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly disrupts traffic flow and can lead to rear-end collisions and unsafe passing.

questions.prompt: Why should you keep a space cushion on all sides of your vehicle?
choices: So you have room to react and an escape path
So you can change lanes without signaling
To drive faster
To stay closer to other cars
questions.explanation: A cushion of space on every side gives you time and room to avoid sudden hazards.

questions.prompt: You may not pass another vehicle when:
choices: A solid yellow line is on your side of the road
A broken yellow line is on your side
You are on a straight road with clear visibility
The road has two lanes in your direction
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, slower traffic should generally:
choices: Keep to the right and use the left lane to pass
Stay in the left lane
Drive in the center lane only
Switch lanes frequently
questions.explanation: Keeping right except to pass keeps traffic flowing and reduces conflicts.

questions.prompt: When is it safe to return to your lane after passing another vehicle?
choices: When you can see the vehicle in your rearview mirror
As soon as you are alongside it
Immediately after you pass its front bumper
Whenever you feel like it
questions.explanation: Seeing the passed vehicle in your mirror confirms there is enough room to return safely.

questions.prompt: On which side should you normally pass another vehicle?
choices: The left side
The right side
Either side
The shoulder
questions.explanation: Passing is normally done on the left where oncoming and passed drivers expect it.

questions.prompt: When a school bus is stopped with its red lights flashing on an undivided road, you must:
choices: Stop in both directions until the lights stop flashing
Pass slowly on the left
Continue if no children are visible
Stop only if you are behind the bus
questions.explanation: Traffic in both directions on an undivided road must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights.

questions.prompt: You should avoid passing when:
choices: Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see ahead
The road is straight and clear
A broken yellow line is on your side
There is no oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Passing on hills or curves is dangerous because you cannot see oncoming traffic in time.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal and check your mirrors and blind spot
Only check your rearview mirror
Change quickly without signaling
Honk your horn
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors and blind spots ensures the lane is clear before you move over.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means:
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe
Passing is never allowed
The road is one-way
You must stop
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line indicates passing is permitted when the way ahead is clear.

questions.prompt: A solid white line between lanes traveling the same direction means:
choices: Lane changes are discouraged
You must turn here
Passing is required
The lane is for parking
questions.explanation: A solid white line discourages or prohibits crossing between lanes going the same direction.

questions.prompt: If you are stuck behind a slow-moving vehicle, you should:
choices: Wait for a safe and legal place to pass
Pass immediately on the right
Tailgate to encourage them to speed up
Pass on a curve
questions.explanation: Patience and waiting for a clear, legal passing zone is far safer than forcing a pass.

questions.prompt: New Hampshire's Move Over law requires drivers to do what for a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights?
choices: Slow down and move over a lane when possible
Stop completely in their lane
Speed up to pass quickly
Honk and continue
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires slowing down and changing lanes away from stopped emergency and maintenance vehicles when it is safe.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Give it a full lane width
Share the lane beside it
Pass it closely
Expect it to move onto the shoulder
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane, and crowding them is dangerous.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
choices: Avoid lingering alongside them
Drive closely beside them
Cut in front and brake
Stay directly behind them at all times
questions.explanation: Trucks have big blind spots where the driver cannot see you, so do not stay alongside them.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Slow down and give them plenty of room
Pass as closely as possible
Honk to make them move over
Speed up to pass quickly
questions.explanation: Bicyclists need extra space because they can wobble or swerve to avoid hazards.

questions.prompt: Bicyclists on the road have:
choices: The same rights and duties as drivers
No legal right to use the road
The right to ignore traffic signals
Priority over all vehicles
questions.explanation: Bicyclists must follow the same traffic laws as drivers and have the same rights to the road.

questions.prompt: Because trucks make wide turns, you should:
choices: Never try to squeeze between a turning truck and the curb
Pass on the side it is turning toward
Move closer during the turn
Assume it will turn from the far lane
questions.explanation: Trucks swing wide to turn, and squeezing alongside can put you in a deadly crush zone.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian who appears to be blind using a white cane or guide dog, you must:
choices: Stop and yield the right of way
Honk to alert them
Drive around them quickly
Proceed without stopping
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians who are blind and using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: If you cannot safely move over for a stopped emergency vehicle, you should at least:
choices: Slow down and pass with caution
Stop in the travel lane
Maintain your speed
Change direction
questions.explanation: When changing lanes is not possible, the law still requires you to reduce speed and pass carefully.

questions.prompt: When you meet farm equipment or a slow-moving vehicle displaying an orange triangle, you should:
choices: Slow down and pass only when safe and legal
Pass immediately regardless of conditions
Tailgate to pressure them
Use your high beams
questions.explanation: Slow-moving vehicles travel under 25 mph, so reduce speed and pass only where it is safe and allowed.

questions.prompt: For drivers age 21 and older in New Hampshire, it is illegal to drive with a BAC of:
choices: 0.08 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.15 percent or higher
questions.explanation: A BAC of 0.08 percent or more is the legal limit for drivers 21 and older in New Hampshire.

questions.prompt: Under New Hampshire's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 can lose their license with a BAC of:
choices: 0.02 percent or higher
0.08 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 face a zero-tolerance standard, and a BAC of 0.02 percent or more is a violation.

questions.prompt: New Hampshire's hands-free law prohibits drivers from:
choices: Using a handheld cell phone while driving
Talking through a hands-free system
Using voice commands
Listening to the radio
questions.explanation: The hands-free law makes it illegal to hold or manually use a cell phone or device while driving.

questions.prompt: In New Hampshire, drivers under 18 may use a cell phone while driving:
choices: Not at all, even hands-free
Only for texting
Only for phone calls
Whenever they want
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 are banned from using any mobile device while driving, even in hands-free mode.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a form of distracted driving?
choices: Texting while driving
Checking your mirrors
Scanning the road ahead
Keeping both hands on the wheel
questions.explanation: Texting takes your eyes, hands, and mind off driving, making it especially dangerous.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your ability to drive?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It improves your focus
It sharpens your reflexes
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow reactions and impair the judgment and coordination needed to drive safely.

questions.prompt: The safest way to avoid distraction from your phone is to:
choices: Set up navigation before driving and put the phone away
Hold the phone low so police cannot see it
Only text at red lights
Use it whenever traffic is light
questions.explanation: Handling your phone before you drive and putting it away keeps your attention on the road.

questions.prompt: If you plan to drink alcohol, the safest choice is to:
choices: Arrange a sober ride home
Drive slowly and carefully
Drink coffee before driving
Wait 15 minutes then drive
questions.explanation: Only time removes alcohol from your body, so a sober driver, taxi, or rideshare is the safe option.

questions.prompt: Legal prescription or over-the-counter medications can:
choices: Impair your driving and make it unsafe
Never affect your driving
Always improve your alertness
Only affect you if combined with alcohol
questions.explanation: Many legal medicines cause drowsiness or slowed reactions that make driving dangerous.

questions.prompt: A first-offense violation of New Hampshire's hands-free law carries a fine of:
questions.explanation: The fine for a first hands-free law violation is $100, with higher fines for repeat offenses.

questions.prompt: In New Hampshire, you must turn on your headlights whenever:
choices: Your windshield wipers are in continuous use
You are driving on a highway only
You are turning
It is sunny
questions.explanation: State law requires headlights on whenever your wipers are running continuously due to weather.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving
questions.explanation: Low beams reduce glare in fog, while high beams reflect back and worsen visibility.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Slam on the brakes
Steer sharply the opposite way
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: Gentle steering and easing off the gas help you regain traction without making the skid worse.

questions.prompt: Black ice is most likely to form first on:
choices: Bridges and overpasses
Sunny straightaways
Gravel roads
Steep hills
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses freeze first because cold air circulates above and below them.

questions.prompt: When it begins to rain after a dry spell, the road is especially slippery because:
choices: Oil rises to the surface and mixes with water
The water is clean
Tires grip better when wet
The road cools down
questions.explanation: Oil and dust lift to the surface in the first rain, creating a slick film until it washes away.

questions.prompt: In heavy rain or snow, you should:
choices: Slow down and increase your following distance
Maintain your normal speed
Use cruise control
Follow closely to see better
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility mean you need more time and space to stop safely.

questions.prompt: If heavy fog or a storm makes it too dangerous to drive, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Stop in your lane
Speed up to get through it
Drive with your high beams on
questions.explanation: Getting fully off the road with hazards on keeps you visible and out of the path of traffic.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning happens when:
choices: Tires ride up on a film of water and lose contact with the road
Brakes overheat
The engine stalls
Tires grip too tightly
questions.explanation: At higher speeds on wet roads, tires can lose contact with the pavement and ride on water.

questions.prompt: When driving at night, you should:
choices: Reduce speed so you can stop within the range of your headlights
Use only your parking lights
Drive faster to spend less time on the road
Look directly at oncoming headlights
questions.explanation: At night you should be able to stop within the distance lit by your headlights.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb lets the curb stop the car if the brakes fail.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
choices: Within the distance marked or signed, never blocking it
Within 5 feet only
There is no restriction
Within 50 feet always
questions.explanation: Parking that blocks a fire hydrant is prohibited so emergency crews can reach it.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a legal place to park?
choices: A marked space away from hydrants and crosswalks
On a crosswalk
In front of a fire hydrant
In a disabled space without a placard
questions.explanation: Park only in legal spaces that do not block hydrants, crosswalks, or accessible spaces.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill without a curb, you should turn your wheels:
choices: So the vehicle would roll away from the travel lane
Toward the road
Straight ahead
Left every time
questions.explanation: Without a curb, point the wheels so the car would roll off the road rather than into traffic.

questions.prompt: Before leaving a parallel parking space, you should:
choices: Signal and yield to passing traffic
Pull out quickly
Back up onto the sidewalk
Honk and go
questions.explanation: Signaling and yielding lets you re-enter the traffic flow safely.

questions.prompt: On a two-way road, you must normally drive on:
choices: The right half of the roadway
The left half of the roadway
The center of the road
Either side
questions.explanation: Drivers must keep to the right half of the road except when passing or directed otherwise.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for persons with disabilities only if:
choices: You have a valid disability placard or plate
You will be quick
No other spaces are open
You turn on your hazards
questions.explanation: Accessible spaces require a valid placard or plate; using them otherwise is illegal.

questions.prompt: When you park on any hill, you should always:
choices: Set the parking brake
Leave the car in neutral
Leave the doors unlocked
Turn off the headlights only
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake helps keep the vehicle from rolling while parked.

questions.prompt: On a limited-access highway, you should never:
choices: Back up or stop in a travel lane
Use your turn signals
Check your mirrors
Stay in your lane
questions.explanation: Backing up or stopping in a highway travel lane is extremely dangerous and illegal.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the New Hampshire written knowledge test for a license?
choices: 40 questions
25 questions
50 questions
100 questions
questions.explanation: The New Hampshire written knowledge test consists of 40 multiple-choice questions.

questions.prompt: What score must you achieve to pass the New Hampshire written knowledge test?
choices: At least 80 percent
At least 60 percent
At least 70 percent
100 percent
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the questions correctly to pass the test.

questions.prompt: To earn a youth operator license, a driver under 18 must log how many hours of supervised driving?
choices: 40 hours, including 10 at night
20 hours total
10 hours total
50 hours, including 20 at night
questions.explanation: New Hampshire requires 40 hours of supervised driving, with at least 10 of those hours at night.

questions.prompt: A youth operator under 18 in New Hampshire may not drive during which hours?
choices: Between 1:00 a.m. and 4:00 a.m.
Between midnight and 6:00 a.m.
Between 10:00 p.m. and 5:00 a.m.
There is no restriction
questions.explanation: Youth operators under 18 are prohibited from driving between 1:00 a.m. and 4:00 a.m.

questions.prompt: When a person under 18 practices driving in New Hampshire, who must accompany them?
choices: A licensed adult at least 25 years old in the front passenger seat
Any licensed driver
A parent only, in the back seat
No one is required
questions.explanation: New Hampshire requires a licensed adult who is at least 25 to sit in the front passenger seat while a minor practices.

questions.prompt: During the first six months with a youth operator license, a driver under 18 may carry:
choices: No more than one passenger under 25 who is not family, unless a 25+ adult is present
As many passengers as seat belts
Only adult passengers
No passengers at all
questions.explanation: For the first six months, the youth operator may carry at most one non-family passenger under 25 unless a licensed adult 25 or older rides along.

questions.prompt: A youth operator may never carry more passengers than there are:
choices: Seat belts in the vehicle
Doors in the vehicle
People they know
Cup holders
questions.explanation: Youth operators may not carry more passengers than the number of available seat belts.

questions.prompt: You should carry your driver license:
choices: Whenever you are driving
Only on long trips
Only at night
Only when leaving the state
questions.explanation: You must have your license with you and show it to law enforcement when asked.

questions.prompt: Before being issued a youth operator license, a driver under 18 must complete:
choices: An approved driver education course
Only the written test
A defensive driving refresher
Nothing additional
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 must complete an approved driver education course before licensing.

questions.prompt: By driving on New Hampshire roads, you give implied consent to:
choices: Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs if lawfully arrested
Random vehicle searches
Paying any fine without a hearing
Waiving your license
questions.explanation: Implied consent means you agree to chemical testing for impairment when lawfully arrested.

questions.prompt: Refusing a chemical test after a lawful DWI arrest in New Hampshire results in:
choices: An automatic license suspension
A warning only
A small fine with no other penalty
No consequences
questions.explanation: Refusing the test triggers an automatic suspension separate from any penalty for the offense itself.

questions.prompt: Driving in New Hampshire is best described as:
choices: A privilege that can be suspended or revoked
An unconditional right
A one-time permission
A guarantee for life
questions.explanation: Driving is a privilege that can be taken away for unsafe or illegal behavior.

questions.prompt: New Hampshire seat belt law requires that:
choices: Every driver and passenger under 18 be properly restrained
Only the driver wear a belt
No one needs a belt under 21
Belts are optional in the back seat
questions.explanation: State law requires all drivers and passengers under 18 to be buckled up or in a proper restraint.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you must:
choices: Stop and exchange information, and report it if required
Leave if no one seems hurt
Drive away to avoid a ticket
Wait only if the other driver insists
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash is a serious offense; you must stop, exchange information, and report as required.

questions.prompt: Accumulating too many points or serious violations can lead to:
choices: License suspension
A reward
A higher speed limit
No effect on your license
questions.explanation: Too many points or serious offenses can cause your license to be suspended.

questions.prompt: When a law enforcement officer signals you to pull over, you should:
choices: Pull over safely and follow the officer's instructions
Speed up to find a better spot far away
Ignore the officer if you did nothing wrong
Stop in the middle of the lane
questions.explanation: Pull over safely as soon as it is safe and cooperate with the officer's directions.

questions.prompt: Young children in New Hampshire must ride in:
choices: An approved child restraint until they reach the required age and size
The front seat only
Any seat without a restraint
An adult's lap
questions.explanation: Children must use an approved child safety restraint until they meet the required age and size limits.

questions.prompt: A conviction for driving while intoxicated (DWI) in New Hampshire typically results in:
choices: License loss, fines, and required programs
A warning only
No penalty for a first offense
An automatic license upgrade
questions.explanation: Even a first DWI usually brings license loss, fines, and mandatory impaired-driver programs.
